headline: European Union Bans Destruction of Unsold Fashion
meta_description: The European Union has made it illegal to destroy unsold clothing and footwear, tackling fashion industry waste at its source.
summary_short: Europe has outlawed the practice of destroying unsold fashion items, forcing the industry to rethink waste and find better alternatives.
body:
The fashion industry has long kept a dirty secret: tonnes of perfectly good unsold clothing and footwear are destroyed each year to maintain brand value and control inventory. Much of this happens in Europe, where mountains of unused products end up in landfills or incinerators.

The European Union has decided this ends now. New regulations prohibit the destruction of unsold fashion and footwear, forcing companies to find alternatives. Brands must now donate, resell, recycle, or responsibly dispose of surplus goods rather than simply throwing them away.

The shift is seismic. It challenges a business model built on scarcity and exclusivity, pushing the industry toward sustainability by legal requirement. Companies are discovering second-hand markets, donation networks, and recycling innovations they might never have explored under the old system. For consumers and the planet alike, it means fewer resources wasted, more items finding useful lives, and a fashion industry gradually learning to operate within planetary boundaries.
